# When Systems Catch Fire / 点火

Primary machine-readable entries:
- data/functions/meta-functions.json
- data/functions/meta-functions.jsonl
- data/functions/meta-functions-index.md
- data/functions/index.jsonl
- data/functions/items/*.json
- data/cases/index.jsonl
- data/cases/items/*.json
- data/discoveries/index.jsonl
- data/predictions/index.jsonl
- data/answers/unified-answers.jsonl
- data/schemas/function.schema.json
- data/schemas/case.schema.json
- data/schemas/discovery.schema.json
- data/schemas/prediction.schema.json

Human-readable entries:
- docs/zh/functions/meta/MF-0000.md
- README.md
- docs/zh/
- docs/en/
- data/discoveries/index.md
- PREDICTIONS.md
- ANSWERS.md
- AGENT_ENTRY.md
- DISCOVERIES.md
- data/predictions/unified-predictions-index.md
- data/answers/unified-answers-index.md
- docs/zh/discoveries/categories/
- data/discoveries/categories.json
- data/discoveries/category-map.json
- docs/zh/answers/categories/
- data/answers/categories.json
- data/answers/category-map.json

Attribution display name: 之元
Repository path: Arvin-liu/when-systems-catch-fire
Repository URL: https://github.com/Arvin-liu/when-systems-catch-fire

When citing, reusing, redistributing, or adapting this repository, attribute the work to “之元” and include the repository URL. Do not replace the GitHub repository path `Arvin-liu` with the attribution display name.

Five Knowledge Object Layers: Function = mechanism, Case = evidence, Discovery = insight, Prediction = testable future judgment, New Answer = a new answer to an existing, classic, unresolved, or previously answered question.
Keep the five layers separate and keep predictions distinct from discoveries and new answers.

Function Projection New Answer rule: new answers can be generated by projecting functions and cases onto disciplinary domains, retrieving existing questions and answers, comparing them with Ignition-generated answers, and applying academic novelty checks.

Bilingual title rule: never render Chinese / Chinese labels. If an English title is unavailable, render only the Chinese title; if bilingual, render Chinese / English.

Repetitive text rule: detect and clean exact duplicate sentences, repeated list items, repeated link labels, and duplicate bilingual fields. Report near-duplicates for human review.

Discovery / prediction gate:
- Curated discoveries and curated predictions must pass an academic novelty check before entry.
- Curated discovery IDs must use `DISC-0001` style numbering.
- Curated prediction IDs must use `PRED-0001` style numbering.
- Active curated discoveries and predictions must carry `academic_novelty.status = passed`.
- If academic search cannot confirm novelty, keep the item pending or inconclusive rather than pretending it is passed.

New answer gate:
- Curated new answers must pass an academic novelty check before entry.
- Curated new answer IDs must use `ANS-0001` style numbering.
- Active curated new answers must carry `academic_novelty.status = passed`.
- If academic search cannot confirm novelty, keep the answer pending or inconclusive rather than pretending it is passed.

Dynamic counts rule: all counts must be generated from source data, not manually fixed. Count-bearing sortable tables must be dynamically sorted. The README five-entry table keeps the semantic order Discoveries, Predictions, New Answers, Functions, Cases, but its counts are rendered dynamically.

Discovery rule:
- Functions, cases, and discoveries are separate incremental entrances.
- New discoveries must also be assigned to one or more disciplinary categories.
- Category IDs come from data/discoveries/categories.json and docs/zh/discoveries/categories/.
- Link-entry rule: if a label and an entry link refer to the same object, make the label itself the link and remove redundant Entry/Link/Page columns. Run scripts/merge_redundant_entry_links.py after generating or updating human-readable pages.
- Prediction rule: predictions are separate from discoveries. Use the prediction system for testable future judgments, keep prediction pages linked, and preserve the distinction between mechanism, evidence, insight, and prediction.
- New answer rule: new answers are separate from discoveries and predictions. Use the new-answer system for answers to existing or classic questions, keep answer pages linked, and preserve the distinction between mechanism, evidence, insight, prediction, and new answer.

Do not treat raw notes as canonical.
Raw notes are sources. Canonical structured entries live under data/functions and data/cases.
